idea.title: Peel It!
idea.hook: Drag from the corner to peel the film off in one clean motion — then the sticker, then the dust underneath — until the whole thing gleams.
idea.hook_ru: Тяните от угла, чтобы снять плёнку одним чистым движением — затем наклейку, затем пыль под ней — пока всё не засияет.
idea.prototype: A shiny new gadget (phone, smartwatch, earbuds case, tablet, sunglasses, handheld, camera, laptop) sits in the portrait center covered by 2-3 layered coverings. The player drags from a highlighted, pulsing start corner along a dotted guide to peel the current layer; a glossy curled flap follows the finger and lifts up off the corner while the gleaming surface is revealed behind it (a progress-mask along the guide path, not true cloth physics; the flap is a shaded curled quad that grows with progress). Layers are removed in sequence: outer protective film -> a printed sticker label -> dust residue (wiped with the same drag, with dust particles). Peel too fast (smoothed speed > MAX_PEEL_SPEED) or stray off the dotted guide band (perpendicular deviation > GUIDE_HIT_W, with a wrong-direction angle check) and the film TEARS, costing a tear and a star. Clear every layer to 100% to finish the gadget and unlock the next; 8 gadgets with a rising-difficulty diagonal guide. Stars (3 for a tear-free clear, fewer with tears) convert to coins that buy META upgrades between gadgets: Steady Hand (wider tear tolerance), Quick Peel (faster cap), Auto-Flatten (smoother lift). Non-timer pressure: a TEAR meter (3 strikes) + clean-% completion target + star/coin scarcity — no timer. WebAudio peel ticks + clean chime.
idea.why_novel: peel verb + layered breakable layers, distinct from the shipped wash/wipe/scrub coverage-reveal games. The portfolio already ships 17_clean_sweep (wipe), 18_ice_cleanup, 89_merge_cleaners, and 103_power_wash (spray coverage-reveal, shipped yesterday). Peel It! introduces a brand-new tactile verb — drag-to-peel a curled flap along a guide — with an off-guide/over-speed TEAR-meter as the failure axis and 2-3 sequential breakable layers (film -> sticker -> dust), directly answering the ice_sweeper comment 'implement 2-3 layered breakable layers, add sounds, maybe meta upgrades'. It deliberately does NOT use a spray/scrub/wipe coverage grid, so it dedups from power_wash and clean_sweep.
idea.non_timer_pressure: tear meter + clean-% target + star/coin scarcity (NO timer): tears accrue on off-guide (perpPx > GUIDE_HIT_W=70 design-px) or over-speed (smoothed speed > MAX_PEEL_SPEED=900) drags; MAX_TEARS=3 fails the gadget. Win requires CLEAN_TARGET_PCT=100 on every layer. Clean tear-free peels earn 3 stars -> COIN_PER_STAR=2 coins, so sloppy peeling starves the upgrade shop (UPGRADE_COSTS tolerance:4, speed:5, autoflatten:7).
idea.lose_condition: gs='GAMEOVER' when tears >= MAX_TEARS (3) on the current gadget; forces a RETRY of that gadget (coins/upgrades kept).

synthesizer_brief: Extend the 100%-liked cleaning/satisfying family with a fresh PEEL mechanic and the requested 2-3 layered breakable layers + meta upgrades; dedup from yesterday's power_wash. This is a portfolio-KNOWLEDGE pick (inside-out, from our own metrics): cleaning is our highest like-ratio genre at 👍11/👎0 (100%), led by Clean Sweep Donut (slug clean_sweep, ~36.2s/play, 20 plays, 👍5/👎0). The build bakes in the comment-theme hard requirements: crystal-clear goal in 5s (first gadget pulses the start corner with an animated arrow + dotted guide and a big '% CLEAN' meter, teaching by doing); mobile-portrait-first (the whole interaction is a single thumb drag on a large gadget filling the portrait frame, with every button — PLAY/NEXT/RETRY/UPGRADE/? — in the lower-center thumb zone, none in top corners); real depth (8 gadgets of rising difficulty + a 3-track meta upgrade shop); and the explicit ice_sweeper request for 2-3 layered breakable layers + sounds + meta upgrades. CTR uses the satisfying_cleanup archetype: before/after contrast with a big curled film flap mid-peel and a gleaming surface revealed underneath.

sources.title: cleaning genre 100% like-ratio; Clean Sweep Donut top
sources.snippet: cleaning 43.9s/play 100% likes; ice_sweeper comment: implement 2-3 layered breakable layers, add sounds, meta upgrades

satisfying_cleanup — before/after transformation, visible peel path, high tactile contrast. Strong portal archetype: Element Playground (satisfying), Zen Mower (satisfying), Trash Master (satisfying), Cut the Rope. Make the cover read as a deeply satisfying mid-peel with a big curled flap.

alternatives.title: Sand Reveal
alternatives.hook: Brush drifting sand off a buried relic to reveal the polished artifact underneath, sweeping along the grain without scattering it back.
alternatives.why_novel: Same before/after reveal satisfaction but via brushing sand off a buried object rather than peeling a film — passed over because the sweep gesture is closest to the already-shipped clean_sweep/power_wash coverage-reveal we must dedup from.

alternatives.title: Rust Scrape
alternatives.hook: Scrape rust and old paint off a metal blade with a precise tool stroke, revealing mirror steel — press too hard and you gouge the metal.
alternatives.why_novel: A pressure-precision scrape with a 'gouge' failure axis is a fresh tactile verb, but it overlaps varnish_restore's restore loop and a press-pressure input is harder to make thumb-friendly on mobile portrait, so peel won.

alternatives.title: Sticker Residue Roll
alternatives.hook: Roll a sticky lint roller over leftover glue residue and lint, picking it up in satisfying strips until the surface is squeaky clean.
alternatives.why_novel: A rolling pickup with a 'roller gets saturated' resource axis is novel, but the roll gesture reads as another coverage-sweep and lacks the one-clean-motion PAYOFF of a curled peel flap, which is the stronger CTR hook.

alternatives.title: Polish Reveal
alternatives.hook: Buff a foggy gemstone or trophy in circular strokes to bring out a blinding shine, building combo as the gleam spreads.
alternatives.why_novel: Circular-buff to reveal shine is satisfying but is essentially a coverage-fill reveal (close to polish_pass) without the breakable-layer / tear-risk tension the ice_sweeper comment explicitly asked for.
